config.name: okta_ocsf_logs-latest_transform
config.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: okta_ocsf_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Authentication (3002)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:10:54.637877

-- Pre-declare locals for performance
local type = type
local pairs = pairs
local os_time = os.time
local string_format = string.format
local table_insert = table.insert

-- Validation helper functions
local function validate_string(value)
    return type(value) == "string" and #value > 0
end

local function validate_number(value) 
    return type(value) == "number"
end

local function safe_get_nested(tbl, ...)
    local current = tbl
    for _, key in ipairs({...}) do
        if type(current) ~= "table" then return nil end
        current = current[key]
    end
    return current
end

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with efficient table construction
    local output = {
        metadata = {
            version = "1.0.0",
            product = {
                name = "Okta Identity Cloud",
                vendor = "Okta"
            }
        },
        class_uid = 3002,
        class_name = "Authentication",
        category_uid = 3, 
        category_name = "Identity & Access Management",
        activity_id = record.uuid or string_format("okta-%d", os_time()),
        type_uid = 300201,
        type_name = "User Authentication",
        time = record.published or (os_time() * 1000),
        status = "SUCCESS", -- Default status
        user = {},
        client = {},
        observables = {}
    }

    -- Efficient field mapping with validation
    -- User context
    local actor = record.actor
    if type(actor) == "table" then
        output.user = {
            account_uid = actor.id,
            account_type = actor.type,
            email_addr = actor.alternateId,
            name = actor.displayName
        }
    end

    -- Client context with nested validation
    local client = record.client
    if type(client) == "table" then
        output.client = {
            ip = client.ipAddress,
            location = {}
        }
        
        -- Efficient geographical context mapping
        local geo = client.geographicalContext
        if type(geo) == "table" then
            output.client.location = {
                city = geo.city,
                country = geo.country,
                state = geo.state,
                postal_code = geo.postalCode,
                lat = safe_get_nested(geo, "geolocation", "lat"),
                lon = safe_get_nested(geo, "geolocation", "lon")
            }
        end

        -- Browser context
        local ua = client.userAgent
        if type(ua) == "table" then
            output.client.browser = ua.browser
            output.client.os = ua.os
            output.client.userAgent = ua.rawUserAgent
        end
    end

    -- Authentication context
    local auth = record.authenticationContext
    if type(auth) == "table" then
        output.authenticationStep = auth.authenticationStep
        output.externalSessionId = auth.externalSessionId
    end

    -- Event metadata
    output.category_name = record.eventType
    output.legacy_category_name = record.legacyEventType
    output.msg = record.displayMessage

    -- Outcome handling
    local outcome = record.outcome
    if type(outcome) == "table" then
        output.result = outcome.result
        if outcome.result == "FAILURE" then
            output.status = "FAILURE"
        end
    end

    -- Validation of required OCSF fields
    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid class_uid"
    end

    if not validate_string(output.activity_id) then
        return nil, "Invalid activity_id"
    end

    -- Clean empty tables
    for k, v in pairs(output) do
        if type(v) == "table" and next(v) == nil then
            output[k] = nil
        end
    end

    return output
end

-- Error handling wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end
